item.title: If you need help with medical bills
item.description:
@Ranlothir 's post yesterday reminded me - if you need assistance with medical bills, Christy Snodgrass https://www.instagram.com/p/CdE8xQ3LdSv has been a warrior in dispensing advice on how to negotiate lower costs. Her instagram feed is filled with a ton of advice and additional tactics on how to help.
segment 2
segment 3 also, @Balikadam shared this video with some great resources: https://imgur.com/gallery/we-should-share-as-many-people-as-possible-who-might-need-medical-bill-help-CtNDGxz
item.dc:creator: lydecker17

item.title: A fully intact mummified Sabre-toothed tiger cub was discovered in Siberia
item.description:
A mummified ice age cub from Siberia is the first known mummy of a sabre-toothed cat, and its discovery is generating ripples of excitement among paleontologists. The mummy’s exceptional preservation provided the first view of what sabre-toothed cats looked like. Written in its soft tissues are clues about where the cat’s muscles were bulkiest and how that may have shaped its hunting style.
Abundant fur and mummified flesh covered the partial corpse, and its face, forelimbs and torso were nearly intact, scientists reported Thursday in the journal Scientific Reports. The cub’s dark brown fur was short but very thick, measuring about 0.8 to 1.2 inches (20 to 30 millimeters) long. Its fur was also found to be surprisingly soft.
“It’s a fantastic feeling to see with your own eyes the life appearance of a long-extinct animal. Especially when it comes to such an interesting predator as the sabre-toothed cat.” These extinct carnivores, which are distant relatives of modern big cats, are known for their long, bladelike canines, which could measure up to 8 inches (20 centimeters) long. basin.
An artist rendering of what a fully-grown one looked like thousands of years ago
The mummy is the first evidence from Asia of the sabre-toothed cat species Homotherium latidens, Lopatin said, though fossilized bones were previously found at sites in the Netherlands and in the Canadian Yukon. Other types of frozen ice age mummies, such as woolly rhinos and mammoths, are known from the Siberian region of Yakutia in Russia. But mummified cats, by comparison, “are extremely rare,”. Before this discovery, there were just two known cat mummies, both cubs of the cave lion Panthera spelaea from Yakutia’s Uyandina River
And it’s not just the anatomy of the Homotherium mummy that makes it so special — the discovery also provides a unique glimpse into the evolutionary history of the entire feline group, Tseng said. Prior genetic analysis of DNA from Homotherium fossils showed that the genus split from other ancient cats about 18 million years ago. Not only is the cub the sole mummified example of the Homotherium genus, “it represents a part of that cat family tree that goes back almost to the origin of the cat family,” he said. “That adds to the mind-blowingness of this discovery.” “Now, we have added the Homotherium cub to this list,” Extracting DNA from the mummy will be an important next step for understanding this species, as will more detailed examination of the mummy’s skeleton, muscles and hair https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-79546-1
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: Trans Girl Tuesday
item.description:
Doing something different again this week. Saw a lot of timeline photos the other day and they were so inspiring. Honestly not sure what to do going forward. I try to post stuff I like and not talk about serious shit, but there's this overwhelming sense of impending dread I'm sure most of us here are feeling that can't be ignored. I'm even worried by highlighting people like this I'm making it easier for the wrong people to find them. These are all easily visible, it's not like I went digging through r/trans_timelines or anything. Still, I don't know. This country fucking failed in epic proportions, and it's not okay. I've lost a lot of faith in humanity, and just like...the universe in general. All I can do for now is put it in the back of my mind and enjoy what I can now, and seeing people overcome whatever bullshit "fate" had in store for them and find a way to be happy brings me joy.
Faith Girlcel is actually the only one here I was following before, she has a pretty wild sense of humor, her posts are a joy to read.
item.dc:creator: MermaidNinja

item.dc:creator: SirYumYum

item.title: After 9 years, I am me.
item.description:
PART 1: see my other posts for Part2: CURRENT UPDATE!!! On 9/8/22 I will celebrate 9 years in transition from assigned male at birth to fully living and succeeding at life as the woman I am. On 9/2 I will celebrate the one year anniversary of My GRS, and I cannot even begin to describe what it feels to finally feel right, to be complete, to be happy and to finally be me.
Edit* Thank you everyone for the warm reception and all the wonderful questions and responses and acceptance! I feel so blessed to be a part of this family!
item.dc:creator: SlanderousLici

item.title: Oh no! The consequences of my own actions!
item.description: In a move that could have been forseen by literally anyone who thought for a few seconds, farmers are realizing that their near-to-slave gravy train is going to end. Personally, I am cynical and suspect we'll be hearing about the bold new plan to force convicts into farming and just fully lean into modern day slavery in America. https://www.newsweek.com/donald-trump-mass-deportation-farmers-1987371
item.dc:creator: AranaDiscoteca

item.title: I FINALLY got my handicap van. I can now go anywhere!
item.description: It's been a long hard road to get here. I was very lucky. The people at United Access really stepped up and helped me. Normally these vehicles start at $40,000 but I got mine for way less. Watch Out World, here I come!
item.dc:creator: pennypoohbear

item.title: We got snow this morning!
item.description:
We don't get much of it here in UK, so i cherish it every time it appears!
I grew up in Poland with proper winters, you know, walking to school uphill and in waist high snow drifts, lol
Makes me feel happy, like a little kid! Have a great day, guys :)
item.dc:creator: LiillooDallasMultipass

item.title: Samus cosplay from Metroid
item.description:
Zero suits are and easy cosplay but I drew the alien larvae insert in the purse and painted both the boots and the gun so that makes me feel like I still put effort into it.
My friend did my makeup and we thought it'd be fun to do it in her armors colors.
This was for the Grand Rapids comicon and was a lot of fun. I plan on doing this one again especially since I have a coworker offering to 3d print her helmet and arm canon for me.
Nyx tax! Big yawn!
item.dc:creator: 00Lochnessa

item.title: I walked 5 miles for a turkey
item.description:
Eleven pounds! Good enough for me! Got it at a turkey giveaway over the weekend. I would save it for Thansgiving but I'm hungry now!
No family, no transportation, no money until Friday. I wore my shoes out but at least I'm not hungry. Let the turkey dishes and sandwiches begin!
item.dc:creator: cooppTvFlyer
